{"type":"standard","title":"Leader of the Opposition","displaytitle":"Leader of the Opposition","namespace":{"id":0,"text":""},"wikibase_item":"Q620110","titles":{"canonical":"Leader_of_the_Opposition","normalized":"Leader of the Opposition","display":"Leader of the Opposition"},"pageid":208564,"lang":"en","dir":"ltr","revision":"1348410822","tid":"bfaf273b-367b-11f1-8cb8-6ea7ce15496c","timestamp":"2026-04-12T14:27:35Z","description":"Leader of the largest faction of a legislature that is not in government","description_source":"local","content_urls":{"desktop":{"page":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leader_of_the_Opposition","revisions":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leader_of_the_Opposition?action=history","edit":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leader_of_the_Opposition?action=edit","talk":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Talk:Leader_of_the_Opposition"},"mobile":{"page":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leader_of_the_Opposition","revisions":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:History/Leader_of_the_Opposition","edit":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leader_of_the_Opposition?action=edit","talk":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Talk:Leader_of_the_Opposition"}},"extract":"The leader of the Opposition is a title traditionally held by the leader of the largest political party not in government, typical in countries utilizing the parliamentary system form of government. The leader of the opposition is typically seen as an alternative prime minister to the incumbent; in the Westminster system, they head a rival alternative government known as the shadow cabinet or opposition front bench. The same term is also used to refer to the leader of the largest political party that is not in government in subnational state, provincial, and other regional and local legislatures.","extract_html":"

The leader of the Opposition is a title traditionally held by the leader of the largest political party not in government, typical in countries utilizing the parliamentary system form of government. The leader of the opposition is typically seen as an alternative prime minister to the incumbent; in the Westminster system, they head a rival alternative government known as the shadow cabinet or opposition front bench. The same term is also used to refer to the leader of the largest political party that is not in government in subnational state, provincial, and other regional and local legislatures.

"}
